title: La exploración de nuestro mundo —
subject: Spanish
description:
Exploring our world
In Unit 2, students move beyond their personal world to how they engage with the wider world.
They do this by exploring options for personal travel and exploration in Spanish-speaking
communities and Australia, and by considering the associated cultural conventions. Students
consider the ways that Spanish and Latin American culture has contributed to the world, and
reflect upon their experiences, compare options and express preferences, while appreciating
diverse cultural values.
Unit objectives
1. Comprehend Spanish to understand information, ideas, opinions and experiences related to
travel and exploration, social customs and Spanish and Latin American influences around the
world.
2. Identify tone, purpose, context and audience to infer meaning related to travel and
exploration, social customs and Spanish and Latin American influences around the world.
3. Analyse and evaluate information and ideas to draw conclusions related to travel and
exploration, social customs and Spanish and Latin American influences around the world.
4. Apply knowledge of language elements of Spanish to construct meaning related to travel and
exploration, social customs and Spanish and Latin American influences around the world.
5. Structure, sequence and synthesise information to justify opinions and perspectives related
to travel and exploration, social customs and Spanish and Latin American influences around
the world.
